---
title: "cc_2026_day9"
author: "Lyndsay Miles"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
30 Day Chart Challenge 2026 Day 9: Wealth
Source: Forbes Fictional 15 (2011) https://www.forbes.com/sites/michaelnoer/2011/03/08/the-forbes-fictional-15-help-us-identify-the-richest-fictional-characters/

```{r}

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(showtext)
font_add_google("Playfair Display", "playfair")
font_add_google("Lato", "lato")
showtext_auto()

# --- Data ---
fictional_15 <- tibble(
  character   = c("Carlisle Cullen", "Scrooge McDuck", "Richie Rich",
                  "Tony Stark", "Jed Clampett", "Adrian Veidt",
                  "Bruce Wayne", "Tooth Fairy", "Thurston Howell III",
                  "Sir Topham Hatt", "Artemis Fowl", "C. Montgomery Burns",
                  "Chuck Bass", "Jay Gatsby", "Lucille Bluth"),
  source      = c("Compound Interest", "Mining", "Inheritance",
                  "Defense", "Oil & Gas", "Marketing",
                  "Defense", "Inheritance", "Howell Industries",
                  "Railroads", "Crime", "Nuclear Energy",
                  "Real Estate", "Racketeering", "Real Estate"),
  net_worth_b = c(34.1, 33.5, 11.5, 8.8, 7.2, 7.0,
                  6.5, 3.9, 2.1, 2.0, 1.9, 1.3,
                  1.1, 1.0, 0.95)
) %>%
  mutate(character = reorder(character, net_worth_b))

# --- Plot ---
ggplot(fictional_15, aes(x = net_worth_b, y = character)) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 0, xend = net_worth_b,
        y = character, yend = character),
    color     = "#1B3A5C",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_point(
  aes(x = net_worth_b, y = character),
  shape  = 21,        # filled circle with border
  fill   = "#F4A261", # gold fill
  color  = "#C17A3A", # darker gold border
  size   = 6,
  stroke = 1.2
) +
geom_text(
  aes(x = net_worth_b, y = character,
      label = "$"),
  size   = 2.8,
  fontface = "bold",
  family = "lato",
  color  = "white"
) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0("$", net_worth_b, "B")),
    hjust  = -0.9, size = 2.8,
    family = "lato", color = "#2d2d2d"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(x = 0, label = source),
    hjust  = 1.1, size = 2.5,
    family = "lato", color = "gray50"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(-12, 42),
    labels = scales::dollar_format(suffix = "B", prefix = "$")
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "The World's Wealthiest Fictional Characters",
    caption  = "Source: Forbes Fictional 15 (2011) | #30DayChartChallenge Day 9\nCreated by: Lyndsay Miles",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text                  = element_text(family = "lato"),
    plot.title            = element_text(family = "playfair", face = "bold",
                                         size = 16, hjust = 0,
                                         margin = margin(b = 6)),
   
    plot.caption          = element_text(size = 9, color = "#2d2d2d", hjust = 0,
                                         margin = margin(t = 12)),
    plot.title.position   = "plot",
    plot.caption.position = "plot",
    plot.background       = element_rect(fill = "#eeeef2", color = NA),
    panel.background      = element_rect(fill = "#eeeef2", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major.y    = element_line(color = "#dddde8"),
    panel.grid.major.x    = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor      = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y           = element_text(size = 9, color = "#2d2d2d"),
    axis.text.x           = element_blank(),
    legend.position       = "none",
    plot.margin           = margin(20, 20, 20, 60)
  )

```


Day 9 #30DayChartChallenge : Wealth
This is a bit of throwback, but I just found out Forbes had an annual list of the richest fictional characters that they posted from 2002-2013. Here's fiction's wealthiest from 2011. 

Data source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/michaelnoer/2011/03/08/the-forbes-fictional-15-help-us-identify-the-richest-fictional-characters/
